title: Opinion: How I used public radio to recruit 20,000 participants for a peer-reviewed study on walking breaks
summary: “With the right structure, Americans will roll up their sleeves, do the science, and trust the result,” Manoush Zomorodi writes.

title: Opinion: New human embryo editing advances require tough conversations on ethical boundaries
summary: Scientists can’t ignore the ethical complexities of new embryo editing advances.

title: STAT+: What an abandoned heart failure trial says about ‘breakthrough’ devices that don’t reach patients
summary: An abandoned heart-failure trial offers a rare window into devices that the FDA deems "breakthrough" but never reach patients.

title: STAT+: FDA drops enforcement against Whoop after it tweaks blood pressure feature
summary: The FDA told wearable maker Whoop last week that it would not take further enforcement action over the company's blood pressure feature.

title: STAT+: Want high-quality generic drugs? One expert has ideas on how consumers can trust their supply
summary: Can the quality of generic drugs in the U.S. be made more consistent? One expert has some ideas on how to do it.

title: Judge rules government can’t stop SNAP dollars from buying candy and sugary drinks
summary: The federal government can't block benefits from the nation's largest food aid program from being used to buy candy, soda, and other sugary drinks, a judge ruled.

title: Pfizer drug acquired in Seagen deal disappoints in lung cancer study
summary: The setback dims the outlook for a medicine Pfizer sees as an important contributor going forward, though some analysts are optimistic about the drug’s chances in another trial.

title: Justice Department announces hundreds of charges in multibillion-dollar health care fraud crackdown
summary: The Justice Department has charged a Texas doctor in an $89 million health care fraud scheme, accusing him of billing insurers for medically unnecessary cardiovascular screening tests.

title: STAT+: Exclusive: Mystery man gets experimental GLP-1
summary: Giving an individual special access to an experimental obesity drug is highly unusual.

title: How Kardigan spun deal leftovers into a $400M IPO
summary: CEO Tassos Gianakakos said having late-stage assets and a team fortified at MyoKardia really resonated with investors. SpaceX's huge public offering didn't hurt either.

title: HHS, responding to China’s rise, moves to fast-track early drug research
summary: A series of newly planned reforms are designed to entice companies to start early trials in the U.S. rather than overseas.

summary: Digital health startup Cadence has raised $100 million at a $1.23 billion valuation to expand its chronic disease management program.

title: Hospitals irate after Eli Lilly follows through on 340B ultimatum
summary: The drugmaker stopped paying 340B discounts to hospitals that didn’t comply with its new paperwork requirements late last week. Hospitals are urging HRSA to step in.

title: STAT+: Gene-editing startup launches with $230 million and a Chinese licensing deal
summary: A new gene-editing startup negotiated contracts with a Chinese company, raised $230 million in funding, and executed a reverse-merger with a preexisting biotech company.

summary: Exclusive: STAT has learned that Eli Lilly and the FDA allowed a 79-year-old to gain extraordinary access to an experimental obesity drug through the agency’s “compassionate use” program.

title: STAT+: A sweeping new AI to detect heart conditions is coming to OpenEvidence
summary: EchoNext, a new AI tool cleared by the FDA, will let doctors use an EKG to easily screen patients for structural heart diseases.

title: Opinion: Ending birthright citizenship could be a public health disaster
summary: If the Supreme Court upholds the executive order ending birthright citizenship, it could “have stark consequences at the intersection of bioethics and public health.”

summary: “There's a particular cruelty buried in the new Medicaid work requirement rules,” write Maria Town and Nicole Jorwic.

title: Trump administration targets disability integration mandate in DOJ memo
summary: A new memo from the Department of Justice seeks to upend landmark disability laws and court rulings.

title: STAT+: Closely watched Pfizer lung cancer drug falls short in clinical trial
summary: Pfizer said an experimental drug it hoped could replace a widely used chemotherapy in one of the most common forms of lung cancer fell short in a clinical trial.

title: Regenxbio to resubmit gene therapy as FDA backtracks on another drug rejection
summary: The agency took another step toward clearing a backlog of Makary-era rejections that appeared to contradict earlier agreements with drugmakers.

title: Sanofi swaps R&D leaders; Merck’s Prometheus deal pays dividends
summary: Houman Ashrafian is leaving Sanofi to pursue a new opportunity. Elsewhere, one of Merck’s prized immune drugs succeeded in a pivotal study and a psychedelics biotech’s shares surged.

title: AbbVie to acquire Apogee, staking nearly $11B on long-acting autoimmune drugs
summary: AbbVie’s largest deal in more than half a decade adds a group of immune disease medications — among them a Dupixent competitor — the company believes to have “mega-blockbuster” sales potential.

title: Bionyra brings in $165M for next-generation immune drugs
summary: The biotech, co-founded by Sofinnova Partners and a former Sanofi immunology R&D head, is testing new biologics against common immune conditions.

title: From episodes to a lifetime: How AI can enable women’s health brands to build a partnership that lasts
summary: Women's health audiences have long memories and finely tuned instincts for what is performative.
